title: Localized route renders hard-coded English copy

evidence.quote:
const steps = [
  { icon: "📄", title: "Create Escrow",                 desc: "Contract is created with roles & terms" },

Escrow Flow

Step-by-step animated view of how a single escrow moves from creation to settlement.

reasoning: The page is mounted under the dynamic /:locale segment in a next-intl configured app, but every visible label and description is hard-coded in English. Non-English locale URLs will therefore render English content instead of locale-specific copy, which violates the route's locale contract and makes the page inconsistent with the rest of an internationalized app.
reproduction: Open the route with any non-English locale segment, for example /es/learn/escrow-flow, and observe that the heading and all step titles/descriptions remain English.
recommendation: Move the heading, intro copy, step titles, and step descriptions into next-intl messages and read them with the app's established translation hook/API for the current locale.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are listed for this feature, and the provided files include no route rendering or locale assertions.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a route/component test that renders EscrowFlowPage within a non-English next-intl provider and asserts that the localized heading and at least one step label come from that locale's messages.
minimumFixScope: Update frontend/src/app/[locale]/learn/escrow-flow/page.tsx to consume localized messages, plus the corresponding locale message files and a focused render test.
